Product: Checking or savings account
Consumer complaint narrative:
On XX/XX/2024 I deposited a check in the amount of {$10000.00} as part of a sale of vehicle. XXXX XXXX was the potential buyer. XX/XX/2024 the funds were made available and proceeded with finalizing the sale of vehicle for a possible pick up XX/XX/2024 or onward. On XX/XX/2024, the buyer contacted me to discontinue the purchase of said vehicle. We communicated several times throughout the morning of XX/XX/2024 to return unused monies. I made several attempts to XXXX and TD Bank to ensure the money was good including to make sure there were no scams or misrepresentations. There were oddities that just raised flags. I called the issuing check institution to verify that this was a legit account, but they were unable to verify the amount of money over the phone ; however, were able to verify that this was a valid and legal banking account. TD Bank call center hub stated that this would be best served at the bank to clear up. At approximately XXXX XXXX, I went to do a wire transfer to refund some monies that was agreed upon. Monies was verified and no red flags on the account at this time. Completed the transaction via deposit as it was a TD Bank customer to another TD Bank customer. It wasn't until later on that same day XX/XX/2024 approximately XXXX XXXX  ( close to end of day ) that I was logging onto my TD Bank mobile app to do a transfer between personal accounts that I learned that the check was returned and my joint bank account was overdrawn XXXX XXXX. I had transactions that I did prior with a small balance that was already in the account prior to depositing fraud check and of course this recent {$3000.00} transaction. I immediately called the TD Bank 's toll free # and spoke with a representative from their call center hub. The same that I called earlier to get clarification. There was no way to reach anyone at the local bank and they had limited transactions that they could do. I am pretty versed in banking financial processes. I explained to the representative the urgency on this and try to figure out why the transaction went through if the check was insufficient. These are usually verifiable within 24-48 ( max ) hours of ACH verified transaction. I reported the fraud to local police department. XXXX XXXX XXXX  dispatched a detective to my home as I was XXXX  from a XXXX  XXXX. I went to the TD Bank office I usually go to right when they opened. I spoke with the young lady, XXXX XXXX, Retail Banker. I had spoken with the day prior ( XX/XX/2024 ) to do the wire transfer explained the situation and she made contact with the Fraud Department at the banking institution. She drafted an affidavit upon their instruction on what transpired. I was not given a copy of the affidavit but trusted the bank would be wanting to take all measures to protect themselves and myself of this scam/fraud scheme. She did explain that because the previous day 's transaction was voluntarily initiated that it may not be reversed. It seems to be that there was a miscalculated accountability on behalf of the TD Bank Financial institution. I trusted TD Bank and that their Fraud Department would have all the indication of the fraud/scam scheme and make a reversal of the transaction, voluntary or not, this individual was not entitled to the monies due to the apparent misrepresentation even if he was not part of it either.

I have been long time and good standing consumer of TD Bank and frankly this is something entirely unforeseeable, unprecedented and unlawful at that let alone not logical at all. My representation of this being a possible scam or fraud should have initiated a red flag at the bank for further investigation the minute I called the call center hub and followed their direction to go to the TD Bank itself. I would have understood that as a point of business including protecting the interests of all parties. As a consumer I did my due diligence and reasonableness to indemnify the bank and myself as soon as reasonably possible within my means including compromising my own health in my XXXX  XXXX. 

To date, I have had 3 associated accounts frozen and pending closure. My primary joint account and 2 family relative accounts my name is on from when my daughters were of young age are subject to closure. There was no prior communication or disclosure of this occurring to either parties. We learned only by accident when my daughter tried to do a transaction on XX/XX/2024 at approximately XXXX XXXX  with her debit card. I reached out to the TD Bank call center hub again to find out what was going on. Upon further researching we learned there was a freeze on the account and they directed me to the TD Bank branch again. At this time, I reached out to XXXX XXXX via email and left a message with a colleague to please call me back as I again was XXXX. No response was returned that day. On XX/XX/2024 I attempted again to call the TD Bank branch but received a voicemail and at approximately XXXX XXXX, I visited the TD Bank store and spoke with XXXX as XXXX XXXX was with another customer. XXXX attempted to contact the Fraud Department to get more information and was not able to make contact after 20  minutes. She was kind enough to say she was trying to restore the accounts relationship after explaining that these accounts were not mine but my daughters in order to resolve the compromised account with monies that was being deposited into one. She stated she will call me back with the findings. XXXX did return the phone call on XX/XX/2024 and stated that the upper department managers at Fraud Department had made a decision to discontinue and sever business with all of us. No further explanation only the vagueness that it was a result of the fraud. I asked if my daughter could come by to close out the account to get monies and XXXX stated that she could do so. My daughter wasn't able to go that same day but made plans for XX/XX/2024. If earlier communication was given, I would have prepared well in advanced including my family if this was going to be a plan of action by TD Bank. This has been an upheaval and caused financial hardship to my entire family. My daughters have no access to the funds that was there including additional funds for other items of business. I tried to even look at the bank policies and agreements. I didn't see anything disclosure on that end or bank discretion on these types of situations. I am not ignorant that the bank has to do their steps to protect themselves and of course monitor suspicious activity. We were not averting any responsibility at all and wanted to continue doing business with TD Bank in the time it took to resolve this. We no longer want that at this time with TD Bank. TD Bank discarded our business relationship very carelessly. We do not want to do business with them where they can not see beyond numbers and look at their patrons/consumers as people. A " one size fits all '' isn't adequate for business operations. Very unethical and poor business practices. I had XXXX give me a faster response in the interception of the fraud in their platforms. There may have been a couple of hiccups but they were very in tune with their consumer base to take steps to quickly resolved. 

Resolution I am seeking is on merit and principle. I want the XX/XX/2024 {$3000.00} money transaction reversed as result of this scam/fraud, subsequent overdraft costs, associated fees, and monies in the bank account returned. In addition, we want there to be no negative impacts on credit reports of all bank accountholders in this unfortunate event. Fraud alerts have been placed including freeze on credit reports. I do understand that there may be associated fees I will be responsible for and will pay those accordingly. Due to the account being closed I was unable to return those fees as planned. I was not ever negating that responsibility ; however, due to TD Bank closing the account monies had to rerouted creating more financial hardship including not being able to meet other obligations. Lastly, educate TD Bank no their practices handling future scam/fraud so as to not have another consumer have to endure this again within reason or with reasonable protocols to protect all parties involved. TD Bank has shown themselves just as untrustworthy and poor goodwill practices as XXXX  XXXX XXXX, XXXX and others who have been named in class actions for improper uses of banking power to their consumer community. 

Emails are not fraud - one is for the advert for the sale of vehicle as well for the two separate TD Bank account notifications. XXXX has scam/phfishing protocols to reduce redundancies for email delivery.
scam_archetype: Fake Check Scam / Vehicle Sale Scam
life_impact_categories: Account Freeze|Loss of Banking Relationship|Health Impact

Consumer complaint narrative: On XX/XX/2023 I received a text message from an unknown number claiming to be my boss, XXXX XXXX. My boss travels a lot and I would not find it uncommon if she had a different number. I was asked to do a favor which involved purchasing XXXX gift cards, and then taking a picture of the gift card and sending it via text to the phone number. The number is XXXX. I first went to the XXXX located in XXXX Massachusetts where I purchased XXXX XXXX gift cards worth {$250.00}. I used my debit card for the purchase. I was asked to send a picture of the card via text and was told that these gift cards were being used for clients. I was told to save the receipts and that I would be reimbursed. I was then asked to purchase XXXX more XXXX gift cards worth {$250.00}. At this time the person on the text told me when they got off the conference call they had great news for me. I was then sent to XXXX to purchase more gift cards. I once again purchased two more XXXX Gift Cards that were worth {$250.00} each. When I went to purchase two more the associate at XXXX proceeded to ask me if I knew who these were going to. I answered that it was my boss that was texting me and that is when she asked if I recognized the number as there have been lots of people getting scammed lately. At this time I realized that could be happening to me. I texted the number back to say that my card was denied. ( which it was not. ) the person on the other line, then asked if I could go to an ATM and get cash. I asked for the person to call me and they stated they could not because they were on a conference call. I then asked for them to text me from the cell phone number that I knew was my boss XXXX XXXX. At this time the person stated they did not have the phone with them. They proceeded to keep asking me if I got more money and that the clients were waiting on me. I blocked the phone number and went to the police station to report the incident. I then called M and T bank to report what happened. My debit card was canceled that night .on Monday XX/XX/2023 my husband dropped off the police report to our local M and T branch in XXXX Massachusetts XXXX We asked for it to be faxed to the investigating team. I called on Tuesday the XXXX XXXX to confirm it had been faxed, which they stated it was. When I checked with the bank on Friday via email, I was told that they denied my dispute and never received the police report. I called the bank to request an appeal on Saturday, XX/XX/XXXX. I also reported the scam to XXXX and gave them the gift card numbers on Monday, XX/XX/2023 but they stated that the gift cards had already been redeemed.

Product: Checking or savings account
Issue: Problem caused by your funds being low
Consumer complaint narrative:
I am filing this complaint against XXXX Bank due to their wrongful handling of fraudulent transactions on my checking account. Despite providing clear evidence that I was a victim of fraud, the bank refused to reverse the charges, leaving me with an overdraft of nearly {$4000.00} and additional charges due to altered/fictitious items. I believe XXXX failed in their duty to protect consumers from fraud and wrongfully placed financial responsibility on me instead of investigating properly. 

What Happened? 
Fraudulent Transactions : On Monday XX/XX/year>, I was contacted by scammers who manipulated me into transferring funds via XXXX XXXX and purchasing prepaid debit cards. 
I realized I was scammed and immediately reported the fraudulent activity to XXXX Bank. 
XXXX Response : Instead of assisting me, XXXX conducted an investigation and determined that no error occurred, despite clear evidence that I was a fraud victim. 
They issued a letter on XX/XX/year>, confirming my claim was denied, holding me responsible for {$4000.00}. 
XXXX also charged my account for Altered/Fictitious Items ( per documents I received ), including a returned deposit fee of {$2400.00} and another set of fictitious check charges. 
These charges resulted in my account being overdrawn by nearly {$4000.00}. 
Why This Is Wrong : XXXX identified these transactions as Altered/Fictitious Items but still placed the burden on me rather than protecting my account. 
They failed to recognize that fraud victims can be manipulated into sending money and used my willing participation against me instead of acknowledging that I was deceived. 
They refused to reverse the fraudulent transactions and overdraft fees, forcing me to come out of pocket to cover a scam I was a victim of.Customer service representatives dismissed my concerns, providing little assistance and treating my case as if I was responsible for my own fraud victimization.

Consumer complaint narrative: I am writing to formally express my concerns regarding Zelle 's handling of disputes, particularly in light of the recent CFPB lawsuit highlighting significant issues with the platform. Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
On XXXX XXXX, 2021, I was contacted by someone on XXXX XXXX looking to buy an item I had listed. I generally use cash for these transactions, but the person asked for my Venmo username, and without thinking, I provided it. A few minutes later, they messaged me that they had accidentally sent me {$2000.00}, which was their rent money. They sent me many frantic messages begging for it back. I was skeptical, and took some time to research whether this might be a scam or not. The clearest advice I found came from Venmo 's help pages, which told me that if a stranger sends you money accidentally, and you see it in your Venmo balance ( which I did ), then it is safe to send back, and that is what they advise you to do. When I later spoke with a number of Customer Service representatives on the Venmo 's fraud line, they advised the same. I saw that my Venmo balance contained the extra {$2000.00}, so I accepted the request for a refund. After sending the money, the person stopped responding to messages and deleted their XXXX account. 
Almost immediately, I realized I had probably done the wrong thing and contacted Venmo again, and asked them to reverse my refund payment. They said I had done the correct thing and it was likely not a scam and they would not reverse my payment. 
On XXXX XXXX, I noticed that my Venmo account now had a negative balance, and that there had been a chargeback filed by the sender. From my online research, I assume their card was stolen by a scammer when the payment was made to me, and when I issued the refund, the scammer sent it to his own bank account. I submitted these details to Venmo, who argued on my behalf with the credit card company, but the credit card company found in the sender 's favor. I am now ( as of XXXX XXXX ) told by Venmo that I owe them over {$1500.00} ( they have already seized approximately {$500.00} that was in my balance at that point ). They have said there is nothing else they can do. I am frustrated and confused that there are no protections of the Venmo Balance, given that some protections would be offered by my bank account or credit card. Because the money was sent to me first, it was withdrawn from my Venmo Balance, instead of a source that might offer me better protections. I am even more outraged that Venmo ( on both its published help pages and Customer Service lines ) offers incorrect guidance, which only results in costs for users, but Venmo takes no responsibility.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
My name is XXXX XXXX ( SSN : XXXX ), Bank of America account number XXXX XXXX XXXX and consumer ID number XXXX. Below is my dispute on this account to remove the inaccurate information about the supposed fraud on my record : On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a phone call from someone who claimed to be my employer from over the summer. I worked as a XXXX  XXXX on the border of XXXX and XXXX XXXX This unknown caller gave details about work such as the location, names, and referenced the fact that I had not been present or paid for the second half of the last pay period. They said that they hadn't paid a few employees, including myself, and they were trying to get on that as soon as possible. Since I had been waiting on that check, this call was not surprising. The only thing that they said that was confusing to me was that they wanted to send me the checks for 2 other employees ( which they named and were actual employees, working at the same XXXX ), and wire it to them in order to save time because they could only process one payment per week. Because they expressed an urgency, and I was under the impression that this was my boss, I cashed all three electronic checks via Bank of America 's mobile app that same day. 

The next day, XX/XX/XXXX, {>= $1,000,000} was withdrawn from my account in order to put my account on hold. I made a call to my bank, Bank of America, and they let me know of this and told me it would take 48 hours in order to do a full investigation on the fraud found. That day, I called back the number of my supposed employer and the other end just beeped, likely because the phone had been disconnected. I reached out to my employers via email and they told me they had not reached out. I made an appointment with the Bank 48 hours later, on Friday, XX/XX/XXXX at XXXX at the Bank of America in XXXX. I showed up for my appointment 10 minutes before and explained my situation in full, including the fraud, and they told me it would take an hour to do an evaluation, and that the bank was closing too soon to complete it. They agreed to give me an appointment the next day on Saturday, XX/XX/XXXX at another location on XXXX XXXX. I, once again, explained my situation in full, including the fraud, but this location explained that they do not do fraud investigations on weekends, and sent me away once again -- still without my money. 

I called the fraud line on the following Monday, explaining my situation in full for the third time, as instructed by the manager at the XXXX XXXX location. I was passed around different departments for several hours before being told that they had not yet begun the investigation because the hold was placed on Friday, when it was, in fact, placed on the previous Wednesday. I waited two more days before calling again and they still weren't finished with the investigation. 

A full week and a half later on Monday, XX/XX/XXXX, I called and they let me know that I was guilty of fraud, so my account would be shut down on the XXXX, and I would be unable to take action until then. I went back to the Bank of America in XXXX on Wednesday, XX/XX/XXXX to file a complaint about the situation and how it was being handled, explaining the fraud once again, but I was turned away because I was no longer a customer, after I had been forced to close my account. 

The same day, I also called Bank of America to handle this situation and I was turned away from help, but after hours on the phone explaining my situation to everyone I spoke to, they agreed to send me the check with all the money on the account that they closed. I thought this was the end of the situation, but I was wrong. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I received my check and 3 days later, before I had the chance to put the money in an account at another bank, I received a letter from Bank of America. This letter notified me that I would never be able to open up a bank with them ever again. This was not any new information. That being said, the letter also said that I would likely have trouble opening up a bank anywhere in the US for the next 5 years, because they reported my fraud to both XXXX and XXXX XXXX XXXX. Both of these security systems oversee many US banks, and would prevent me from opening a bank account if I applied. I reached out to XXXX, who launched their own investigation after I submitted additional information. They reinvestigated, and found that the results were inconclusive a few weeks later on Tuesday, XX/XX/XXXX. The individual I spoke with expressed the need for additional supplementation, like an affidavit or a police report. The person on the phone said that I would receive a letter including information on how to go about this process in the mail in a few days. When I received the letter a week and a half later, on Tuesday, XX/XX/XXXX, it did not include such information. I reached out on Friday, XX/XX/XXXX of the following calendar year to state my confusion, to which the person I spoke to expressed that they were never sending such information. I spoke to an officer from the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX the same day in the hopes to overturn the ruling made by XXXX. 

At this point, I am reaching out to XXXX XXXX XXXX, because they have not fully resolved the problem with the information provided. XXXX has removed their false information, however, XXXX  refuses to remove the flag for *fraud* because it does not hold me personally accountable. That being said, the language and style of presentation on my consumer report does not properly represent the situation. I would like for this situation to be rectified.

Last week the closing attorney wired the XXXX payoff funds to PHH and PHH has confirmed receipt, however, they claim that they payoff funds are short since PHH has been accruing interest since XXXX, the date they received and subsequently lost the original pay off check. I have complained there should not be any extra interest since they lost the check in the first place and so some group at PHH is looking into it. When I called, they again said the payoff was short and that they can not apply the funds in any way until the issue with the interest is resolved, which last week I was told could take 20 days. They are a terrible company. Given that the consumer has no say in who the servicer is, being forced to deal with them has been a nightmare and they should not be allowed to service mortgages at all. Just check any review of them. I am beyond livid that there is no one legally responsible for making sure a refi loan is paid off and that I spend hours trying to get someone at PHH to handle this. They are absolutely the worst servicer I have ever dealt with.

Consumer complaint narrative:
Yesterday ( XX/XX/year> ) I received an email from Capital One asking me to verify my information to complete registration for a Capital One QuicksilverOne credit card by sending them " my XXXX most recent consecutive bank statements, my XXXX most recent consecutive pay stubs or earnings statements and my XXXX and/or Form XXXX from the most recent tax year. '' I've never used Capital One and I've never had a credit card. I also received an email from XXXX saying that there was a new installment loan inquiry on my credit report. I looked into my account and saw that there was a pending inquiry for Capital One. I tried to call Capital One this morning, but they said I didn't have an account attached to my name or social security number. I asked them about the application number that was on the email they sent me and they said that only the application department could check on that, so I have to wait until they're open to talk to them. I've yet to call XXXX, but I'm planning on it today. 

I believe that this could've happened because of my run-in with a scammer a few months ago. 

Around the end of XXXX, someone on XXXX messaged me pretending to be a secondary account for someone I knew and they asked if I could help them " get back into their main account. '' I gave them my phone number because they claimed that it was the only way I could get them the help they needed. From there, they messaged my number and asked me to give them codes that I was being sent from XXXX and XXXX, they claimed that this was only related to their situation and had nothing to do with my account. 
From there, they logged me out, changed the email address associated with my account, and asked everyone in my direct messages for money. I was able to regain the account with the " request help from friends '' feature. Near the begining of every month since then, XXXX suspended that account for trying to scam people but I was usually able to get back into it after verifying the account with a video of myself. Unfortunately, the account was permanently taken down a couple weeks ago because of this incident, I don't know why my appeal didn't work this time. 

I believe the hacker was somehow able to create a clone of my phone to get all my information off of it, or at the least, found a way to get a hold of my SMS message history which is the only place I've ever mentioned my social security number. 

Since the initial XXXX hacking situation, I've changed my phone number, factory reset that phone, and switched to a new one ( because of unrelated problems with the battery. )

I asked for a website to visit the business but they don't have one. I told him I couldn't find them on XXXX  and he said law offices are not on XXXX. He was emphasizing that a subpoena was sent to me at my home and that I had to speak to an attorney. He forwarded my call ( I didn't take note of her name but she confirmed she was not an atty ). She said this was a debt collection from Capital One bank for a balance owed from XXXX. It feels like such a breach of privacy. I have already put a fraud alert on my credit to take precautions because I've spent the last 13 years building a solid credit history. My daughter also filed a complaint w/ XXXX  as well as the FTC XXXX Thanks for hearing me out here and looking into all of this. Attached to this complain is the voicemail that was left for me.

Sub-product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
Issue: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
Sub-issue: Card was charged for something you did not purchase with the card
Consumer complaint narrative:
Yesterday I was the subject of a sophisticated phone scam resulting in a fraudulent charge of XXXX {$3300.00} on my American Express card relating to an unauthorized e-commerce transaction on what appears like an online website in XXXX XXXX. 

I received a call at XXXX on XX/XX/31 from Ph. # ( XXXX ) XXXX : my cell phone displayed American Express as the caller from that number. The scammer introduced himself as an American Express ' employee needing to verify two charges on my American Express card which he claimed had just occurred in Texas : one for {$720.00} and one for {$410.00}, both relating to International Travel and Tourism. I declined both charges as being unauthorized : I explained I'm based in XXXX and I had not traveled to Texas nor had I incurred those two charges. He then asked for additional details on my card in order to be able to decline the two charges : he asked for last 5 digits of my Amex card, last 4 digits of my SSN, front and back security codes on my Amex card, and my mother 's maiden name. He then claimed that American Express was going to introduce a two-step verification system in order to use my card out-of-state and internationally going forward. At that point he said I was about to receive an email with a verification code from American Express as part of the new two-step verification process and that I should read the code back to him. At XXXX I received the email from American Express with the verification code and I read the code back to him. Immediately after ( at XXXX ) I then received another email from American Express asking to review a charge of XXXX MYR from XXXX XXXX and showing that this charge had been approved : this charge - which I had never authorized and is fraudulent - immediately appeared in my American Express online account as a charge for US {$3300.00}. 
When I received the email from American Express at XXXX asking me to review the charge and saw the USD charge appear on my American Express online account I asked the caller what that was about and he claimed that it was not a real charge but that the email was only meant to show how the two-step verification process would work when using my card for foreign transactions. He also told me that the two original charges he had called to alert me of would post on my American Express account the following day but they would be declined. At that point he ended the call ( which lasted a total of approx 29min ).

I then immediately called American Express ( at XXXX ) to verify the legitimacy of the call I had just received but they told me on a recorded line that they had no record of the two transactions for {$720.00} and {$410.00} while on the other hand they did see a charge for US {$3300.00} from XXXX XXXX . I explained to American Express the scam I had just been the subject of and notified American Express that the charge from XXXX XXXX ( apparently an online ecommerce website in XXXX XXXX ) was fraudulent and unauthorized. 

The charge is now pending in my American Express account while American Express investigates further. Meanwhile, I have reported the fraud to the Federal Trade Commission ( FTC Report # XXXX ).

Consumer complaint narrative:
Subject : Demand for Explanation and Refund Following Unjustified Denial of Dispute for Unauthorized Charges Dear CashOut Dispute Resolution Team, Case # XXXX and I am writing to address a serious issue regarding unauthorized charges made to my account through your platform. I have repeatedly submitted disputes concerning these transactions, seeking resolution through proper channels. However, each of my disputes has been denied without explanation or evidence substantiating the legitimacy of these charges. This lack of transparency raises serious concerns about the integrity of your decision-making process. 



Issue Details : 1. Over the past several months, I have noticed unauthorized transactions conducted through CashOut. Each of these transactions was made without my consent or authorization. 

2. I filed disputes for each transaction and provided evidence to demonstrate my lack of authorization. Despite this, all disputes were denied, and I received no explanation for the denials. 

3. During this period, I was also informed that my account was being closed due to alleged violations of terms. I have made it clear that my primary concern is not the account closure but the explanation for denying refunds on unauthorized transactions. 



My Requests : 1. I request a detailed explanation for the denial of my disputes, along with documentation substantiating the legitimacy of each charge. 

2. I ask that you thoroughly investigate each unauthorized charge and refund the appropriate amounts to my account. 

3. I expect an official response within 14 days from the receipt of this letter. In the absence of a satisfactory response, I will have no choice but to file a complaint with the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) and consider pursuing legal action. 



I hope CashOut will take appropriate measures to resolve this issue and restore fairness. I believe that refunding unauthorized charges is an essential part of your responsibility to your customers. 



Sincerely, XXXX is respond from Cash out : Thanks for reaching out but we dont currently monitor this email. For help and to contact Support, open your Cash App icon Account was closed and i can used CASH icon No answer for my simple question : .why decline my despute for fraud charge. from my bank account This result of my 3 month conversation with Csh out Hi XXXX, This is XXXX, the Cash App Support manager on duty, with whom you were just speaking. It was my privilege to serve you today. 

I have reviewed your case and I can confirm the information previously provided to you was correct. We conducted a review of your account. Based on that review, we have found activity that violates our Terms of Service ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ), including payment activity along with other potential violations. 

As a result, your Cash App account has been deactivated and you will no longer be able to use your account or its features. 
All features, including the Cash Card have been permanently disabled. Additionally, Cash App has not disclosed a reason for your account closure to me. There are no further details to provide and no further action can be taken. 

You may also consider reviewing our Terms of Service ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ) under Section XIII. OTHER LEGAL TERMS, 8. Termination of Account : " We can terminate or refuse your access to the service at any time, for any reason. '' At this time, I do not have any new information to provide regarding this issue. I really do wish I had a more satisfying outcome to share with you, and I understand the information provided thus far may not present a resolution to the issue at hand. 

Regarding previously denied disputes, I am transferring your case to a specialty team for handling. They will provide the documentation upon which our denial was based. 

Please have a good rest of your day!

Consumer complaint narrative:
I am not sure if this warrants a complaint, but I am checking to see if this is something that can be addressed via policy or if a company can be pressed to change a company. I have a series of specific problems. 
I was foolish and accidentally allowed a company I should not have to start billing my credit card regularly. They did not provide what they said they would. I initiated a dispute with them, but I had a good idea it would be hard to stop them from continuing to use my credit card. I tried multiple ways to get the credit card to not allow the amounts to be charged anymore. This wasn't a small fee -- it was {$1000.00} a month. ( It was a service that was supposed to help me grow a business, but it was not doing what they verbally said they would do. ). 

But the Discover customer service agent on Friday XX/XX/XXXX said they had a policy of not disallowing a merchant to go ahead and continue to use my card. I was flabbergasted at this so I called up on XX/XX/XXXX and said that I would simply close my account and pay off my bill rather than have to allow the situation to continue. Then the costumer service agent, XXXX, became very accommodating and said of course they wouldn't allow a purchase that wasn't something I wanted. They said they'd stop the payment but indicated that it would be possible, in a year 's time, that I'd have to renew the ban on this merchant. This took about an hour but eventually I was assured that there was a block on this particular merchant. After that, I went ahead and engaged with the merchant, who said they would not longer charge me. 

But XXXX and behold, the merchant didn't stick with their word, and neither did Discover. I had even frozen the account, just in case, but I went back today XX/XX/XXXX and found that Discover had indeed allowed the payment through. I was shocked. 

I have now completely closed the account. They verbally assure me there will be no future payments accepted because it is actually closed. I suppose I have to believe them, though I can't trust anything the agents say over the phone, it seems. I even have a letter from them saying that they had blocked the merchant, so a written letter is also no guarantee. I spoke with a supervisor, who went by the name of XXXX, and who basically said I could dispute the {$1000.00} but that it was essentially my fault for allowing the merchant access to my card. I did start a dispute on the payment, and actually decided to dispute all the prior payments as well, but I am also angry that Discover made this so difficult for me to do. ( To be clear, not disputing per XXXX but they refused to block the merchant at all. I think that is a terrible policy. ) Is there anything that can be done about this? I do not believe that Discover should have a policy that lends itself so easily to defrauding their own customers, and I said so to every single customer agent I spoke to. 

I was very foolish. My XXXX XXXX XXXX could see the whole thing was a scam, but I didn't want to believe that. But I do not think that Discover should have sided so fully and wholeheartedly with the merchant when this was such a large amount and this could have gone on indefinitely. Please let me know if there is anything that could be done about this in the future or if there is any way to push for a change in how credit cards treat their customers. 

Thank you.

Consumer complaint narrative: I became victim of a scam last XXXX by a company called " XXXX '' through which I invested about {$13000.00} on cryptocurrency via CRYPTO.COM. They used all types of incentives to have me put in my money. All my transactions had been from my XXXX accounts through Crypto.com to their fake digital wallet on my profile. I realized that I was being scammed when I was told to pay taxes on the " fake '' earnings that were posted in my account on their website. Knowing that only the IRS has the authority to collect tax on investment earnings, I started digging more and found out the whole platform was fake. I confronted them and they threatened to freeze my account. I reached out to XXXX  for assistance with no success. " XXXX '' cleverly orchestrated a prevalent scheme of deception to lead people to invest significant sums while knowing that those would-be investors would ultimately lose the money, they had entrusted to it. The overall purpose of the scheme was to target and defraud people who are often inexperienced and nave, in pursuance of illicit wealth through various fraudulent representations. I did not know, and through the exercise of reasonable diligence could not have discovered, the fraud that was being perpetrated upon me by the Company. As a result of this, I was deceived into transferring my funds for investment returns that were never delivered. I will certainly never receive any monetary value for the investments considering the way the Company had their scheme rigged thus causing significant economic damage to me. I put in claims through XXXX and provided proof of all the fraudulent activities that had been a victim of, unfortunately they denied XXXX of them. I hired a XXXX party company, XXXX XXXX XXXX to help me build a case against both XXXX and Crypto.com. XXXX has not been responsive at all and XXXX completely denied all the claims. The 3rd company promised they would do everything to help me get my money back but I have not heard from them for about 3 months. I also paid the company a retainer to start the process. We entrust Banks to safeguard our hard earned money. Financial institutions are well aware of the scope and nature of cybercrimes as related to cryptocurrency and the risks that these pose to their clients, who, in contrast, mostly have limited knowledge of these dangers. Scam victims have relatively little freedom of reactivity ; under such circumstances, they respond immediately, instinctively, and invariably to the specific demands of the perpetrator. Being able to consciously refrain from reacting in such a way is extremely difficult and sometimes impossible. This complex issue has caused substantial harm to me and other people who have been victims of the same schemes. Those institutions should implement controls to protect people like me with no investment knowledge as we are the main preys of those predators.

Consumer complaint narrative:
On XX/XX/2024, I made an online purchase from a seller on the website XXXX for XXXXXXXX XXXX which amounted to {$170.00}. 

On XX/XX/2024, I received a tracking number for this purchase. Upon checking the tracking number, I noticed the originating scan on the XXXX tracking number was in XXXX, MA, but the seller was located in California. This was my first suspicion. 

On XX/XX/2024, I received an email that the item was delivered. The item was not delivered. I checked all along my house, yard , street, and asked my neighbors if they may have received it by accident. It was nowhere to be found. When I checked the tracking number, it stated it was received by someone unknown to me named XXXX. I immediately opened a dispute with XXXX because at this point I knew something was wrong. 

After going back and forth with XXXX, stating that the item wasnt delivered, I realized I wasnt going to get support from a human being. All responses were canned responses from bots that were not trying to solve the issue, and after many frustrating responses, I gave up. 

At that point, I then called XXXX to get the scoop on the tracking number and found out where it really went ( and came from ). The XXXX rep I spoke to, XXXX, was able to dig up all the relevant information and send it to me in an email. I will attach the email as a document to this complaint. 

From this information via XXXX at XXXX, the seller from XXXX simply found and inputted a random tracking number and used that as proof to XXXX that he shipped it. However, this is a tracking number that the cable company XXXX XXXX used for a shipment to someone in the same town ( different zipcode! ) as me at XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX! 
FYI, my address is XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX... 

I tried one last time to get XXXX support to help me, so I emailed them the information I got from UPS. XXXX support still did not assist, saying that the transaction is closed and there's nothing they can do to help me. 

Apparently this is a very common scam ( as I am now learning ) wherein the seller mines for a tracking number going to the same zip code - although the seller couldnt even do that right, as its not even my zip code! 
They use that stolen tracking number so that XXXX sees it as delivered to the same town. XXXX then washes their hands of it and tells the buyer to pound sand. 

I have now opened a dispute with Capital One, but they have closed the dispute parroting the XXXX line of " well, the tracking shows it was delivered! '' I have re-opened the dispute with Capital One and added additional evidence gleaned from the email I received from the XXXX representative. The case number with them is XXXX. 

Furthermore, if XXXX states that the buyer left a review for the seller so they mustve received the item and been happy with it!... I'll also attach my review of the seller to this complaint so you can see what I said.. It's not positive. 

I will also attach CapitalOne 's initial denial of the dispute claim.

I showed the agent the emails on my phone ( attached to this complaint ) and wiring instructions from the scammer and explained how I verified the beneficiary and he had no doubts. I told him that I called one of the numbers on the email and it was out of service. When I called the other number it got through to someone and I explained what I was calling for and asked to be transferred to the paralegals name at the law firm and then got hung up on. I then told the agent that due to no calls getting through I emailed the scammer asking the amount to verify that this person had access to the closing disclosure, which was a secure document. The agent said that was a good way to check and asked if the account name on the wiring instruction matched with the attorney 's name at the law firm and I said yes, which is factually true. The agent also asked if the addresses matched and I said yes, which is factually true. The agent then continued to wire the funds to the scammer using the wiring instruction under my consent. The bank agent in this case should have given due diligence to ensure the consumer is not falling victim to a scam. The narrative shows that there were instances where the bank agent could/should have recognized scam characteristics. Also the agent should have done more preventive care. A first time home buyer who is not aware of mortgage wire fraud would not know if they are being scammed. The bank should be well aware of mortgage wire fraud to help their clients ensure they are sending the funds to the rightful beneficiary. I informed the agent that I could not get a hold of the law firm administering the closing over the phone and that should have caused the agent to advise not to wire until I can get on a call with the law firm. I paid a service fee of {$30.00} and have been a customer for 6 years. Bank of America could have helped in preventing me from wiring {$23000.00} to a scammer and they should improve their services to better protect their customers from scams. All of Bank of Americas action since has been that they sent a recall and that there is no guarantee of getting any of the money back into my account so file a police report. They said they will contact me with updates, but only update me when I call them which consists of holding for an hour.

Product: Checking or savings account
Consumer complaint narrative:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a text message from TD bank asking to confirm or deny a {$310.00} purchase at XXXX. I replied no. I do receive these text messages periodically which makes me feel comfortable that TD bank is assertive with fraud. An hour after I received this message, I received a phone call from XXXX XXXX XXXX. I know and trust this number as it is TD banks fraud department. The man on the other end professionally explained himself as a Td bank representative with the fraud dept and explained there was a lot of fraudulent activity on my account and it appeared someone was in my online banking making zelle transactions. I did not give any information to this representative ( ie social, login info, passwords ). He did ask that I read back codes being sent for confirmation purposes, which again I trusted. This is a form of confirmation and authentication that my phone provider ( XXXX ) uses as well, so again I trusted this. I do not know TD banks policies and procedures ( if they text, call, etc ). 

I was then told to confirm transfers being sent to XXXX XXXX. The rep explained they are on the other end of the fraud intercepting it in their database. Unfortunately, I was scammed out of {$3500.00}. I was unaware of the term spoofing and that someone can call you and have ANY number appear on your phone and in this case it was the fraud departments. After a police report and two denied claims later, I feel I need a different approach. I have contacted XXXX XXXX office who advised to file a complaint here and with the attorney general as well. I am not a stupid woman. I work in healthcare and am not naive. I learned an extremely expensive lesson on XX/XX/XXXX. However, I have been a TD bank customer for 16 years and TD bank has not offered any type of assistance and/or rectification. I tweeted my experience in an effort to include the public. I did receive a public message from TD bank on XXXX inviting me to contact them personally and we can discuss my matter. It has been over a month and no one has responded to me. The reasoning for my denied claim was due to authorization from the account holder, myself. However, it was under false pretenses. My cyber security was breached. I merely gave a confirmation to what was already going on. This was not a random number or a man calling from XXXX asking for my social security number. It was such a legitimate scam and I am so angry with myself. When I called to file my second claim on XX/XX/XXXX ( after my first claim was denied ), I was told by the representative this is a very popular scam right now. This frustrated me because I think TD bank customers should have been notified of this and this is where TD bank needs to hold some accountability as well. I reached out to the CEO offices and did receive a call back in XXXX from a woman named XXXX and expressed my concerns. She told me there is something in the works to get the word out to their customers about this impressive scam. This is too late for me, unfortunately. I am disgusted with TD bank and how they have treated a loyal customer of 16 years. Banks are insured for a reason and I do feel my complaint holds merit. Yes, I did authorize this scam. However, TD bank failed as well. I do not think it is right they arent holding any accountability in this. They didnt warn customers of what they called such a popular scam. 

I went to XXXX bank to open new accounts and I explained to them what happened and theyre response was, they didnt offer any kind of help after being a customer for so long? They are willing to lose customers rather than do the right thing. I just want my money back. This is a difficult time for people and to offer nothing put a bad taste in my mouth with TD bank. It was insulting as a customer and a person during this pandemic especially to have had nothing offered. At this point I feel I am owed even more money due to the hassle, but I just want was taken from me. TD bank does not care about their customers and that was very evident during this whole experience.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
I made a purchase for XXXX XXXX XXXX kits on XXXX for {$2000.00} USD on XX/XX/XXXX. On XX/XX/XXXX, my package arrived in XXXX while I was out of state. I video called on the XXXX and asked my daughter to open the package. When she brings the package to the phone, it is much smaller than a box that would fit XXXX, let alone XXXX XXXX XXXX kits, and she expresses it feels like there's nothing in it. But there was definitely a small object that rattled inside when the box was shook. We began screen recording the opening. There was nothing but a small plastic hook in side. 

The item was supposed to have purchase protection but I didnt trust XXXX having never done business with them, knowing the price was too good to be true and having heard horror stories. So I purchased it with XXXX layers of protection. XXXX, Paypal and XXXX. 

I immediately reached out to XXXX. The site seemed like it wanted me to mark that I received the items to open a dispute, so I instead spoke to a live agent. They marked the item as shipped and it still wouldn't let me open a dispute. The service rep opened XXXX and directed me on where I could check the status. When I logged in it briefly said the transaction was disputed, but there was nothing listed in the disputes section they had directed me to and I had received no confirmation of a dispute being opened. Then the dispute status also disappeared. So I reached out to PayPal. Also on the XXXX and filed a dispute. 

Paypal seems to be run 100 % by an AI system these days. So I explained all of this to Paypal and they just kept asking for more info. Before any finding in my favor they started telling me to send it back. They literally want me to send this plastic hook back. Fine, whatever. I asked can I get the address in English? I no longer have the empty box, someone threw it out, and my video is too blurry to make out the address. No response. I can't see sellers responses, which seems unfair... how do I defend if they are lying? But, maybe the seller sees my questions and responds but only Paypal sees it? XXXX. I know that I asked for the address XXXX  times. At XXXX point they wouldn't let me add anything but a tracking number. I had to enter XXXX to bypass it so I could further communicate. At another point it required me to upload something in order to communicate. I sent a pic of my dog. I don't know what else to do. It's going on a month. They keep changing the resolution date. All I am getting is generic responses. I reached out in the AI chat and asked for the address and it had no idea what I was saying. They forwarded the convo to a live agent who will get back to me. Maybe. 

Frustrated with how Paypal is handling this, I went to dispute with XXXX. There is an incomplete dispute on the transaction. Seems Paypal contacted them/opened it? So I can't dispute it through them at this moment. The funds are temporarily reversed and everyone seems to be waiting on Paypal. 

Thanks to Paypal I just learned an easy scam. Sell an item at a price that is too good to be true, send a small random object, don't include a return address and demand your item back for a refund. I feel like I should publicly promote this in order for them to make the necessary changes to their dispute process. 

Everytime I respond, they say they will review the info and get back to me and they never do. The dispute process is a time wasting scam. It seems that when their generic responses don't work, they just wait to see who gives up responding first. I have been a Paypal customer for XXXX years. There was an issue with my last dispute as well. This isn't buyer protection. This is a racket. They weren't always like this. 

My last dispute was for {$13.00}. I ordered food from XXXX Eats and didn't receive a {$13.00} dollar add on item. I disputed on XXXX Eats and they offered me a {$5.00} credit which I thought was ridiculous, so I contacted Paypal. Paypal found in my favor and refunded the {$13.00}. Kept the tax though. This isn't the first time either. Next time I ordered from XXXX Eats, they charged me the {$13.00} again. I disputed it and this time Paypal found in their favor because it was in line with my previous transactions. I provided the receipt so this shouldn't have happened if someone was actually reviewing the case. I demanded the evidence they used to find in their favor and they ignored me. Guessing they didn't denand evidence from XXXX Eats. I again demanded it and rather than give it to me they reversed the charge. There was no evidence. This is nothing but AI. Basically, I usually order from XXXX, where tips are expected and allowed. It shows up as XXXX transactions. XXXX for the food and XXXX for the tip. This time, I ordered in XXXX. XXXX doesn't allow tips for delivery drivers so it isn't even an option when ordering. There was no investigation. They had no evidence the {$13.00} transaction was valid. I ended up deleting my XXXX Eats account. In case you haven't noticed, I think XXXX XXXX and Paypal need to be investigated for this. XXXX Eats shouldn't get to recharge people for disputes they won. 

Back to the XXXX guns. I'm sure they could request a receipt from the seller showing the size, weight and tracking number for the package. It is literally on the package. I'm sure they can send me the shipping address if they want the hook back so badly, better yet a preapid shipping label. They are trying to keep over {$2000.00} because I am unable to return a XXXX cent hook due to them refusing to provide the address. They are keeping this pending and prolonging the case resolution which is preventing me from disputing with the more competent XXXX XXXX. 

Buyer protection shouldn't be this complicated. 

If they say they are reviewing the case, someone should actually be doing that. AI doesn't count as a review. 

Return addresses should be provided through the Paypal system so that there is no confusion or disputes about the seller receiving it. How does Paypal know the address on the box is the address on file? They can easily claim it wasn't received or sent to the wrong address. 

Live agents should be available. 

Buyers should be able to see sellers responses and defend themselves from misrepresentations. 

There should be a XXXX to 14 day deadline to resolve disputes. Like banks. It seems Paypal is allowed an indefinite amount of time to prolong these disputes.

Link below : XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX It described a remarkably similar situation to what we've just encountered, in which Bank of America appeared to unlawfully and unfairly discriminating permanent residents who are citizens of another country. After being a loyal customer of Bank of America for many years, we feel that this kind of treatment is completely unacceptable.

Product: Student loan
Issue: Dealing with your lender or servicer
Sub-issue: Received bad information about your loan
Consumer complaint narrative:
On XX/XX/24, I received an email from loan servicer MOHELA with the subject line " Student Loan Rebate Information. '' The body of the email included the following statement : " Congratulations! The specified loan ( s ) have qualified to receive a rebate. The electronic information, which can be found in your MOHELA account, provides more information. '' I then tried to log into my MOHELA account, but the servicer had recently transitioned to a new platform and I had difficulty creating an account. When I finally succeeded in creating an account through the new MOHELA website, I was able to access a letter with slightly more information than the email. The letter ( attached ) lists two XXXX XXXX XXXX totaling {$7500.00} which I previously paid off. Below this information was the following statement : " Benefit Type : Rebate. Benefit Amount Received : XXXX. '' I called MOHELA to try to find out what this meant ( i.e., how to pursue a " rebate, '' but after waiting on hold for 56 minutes I was told that " unfortunately that letter was generated in error during the transition over to the new platform. '' In other words, a message of " just kidding/sorry. '' When I asked why I hadn't received an email yet stating that this had been sent to me in error, the person simply apologized and said that they hadn't gotten around to doing that yet. When I asked if MOHELA planned to post this information to their website or notify the news media so that other borrowers could find out that they are not eligible for a rebate without waiting on hold for 56 minutes during their respective work days ( I want to emphasize that I now need to work an hour later tonight thanks to the time lost in resolving this incident ), the person reverted to their canned response ( i.e., " we're just telling the people who are calling about those letters that the letter was generated in error '' ). The attached XXXX forum shows that I am far from being alone in facing this issue -- MOHELA has sent these misleading emails and letters to countless individuals, all apparently in error. This is outrageously unacceptable, and it's no way to run a federal student loan servicing company. 

There is a lot of uncertainty around student loan forgiveness right now, and there are new developments with each passing day. When a federal student loan servicer sends an official communication, it should be accurate and not misleading. You can not email a borrower to say, 'congrats on your eligibility for a rebate ' only to elaborate in a letter ( which can only be accessed by logging in to a new platform which is actively transitioning and thus error-prone/buggy ) that the borrower is eligible for a rebate of {$0.00}. This is emotionally manipulative and confusing. There are enough scams out there as it is -- I receive calls all the time promising me loan forgiveness or rebates -- I should NOT need to worry about getting scammed or misled by my federal student loan servicer. 

Furthermore, official communication about student loan payments should never contain emotionally charged statements such as " Congratulations! '' This is manipulative because it makes light of the serious financial burden presented by student loans. A student loan rebate can instantly change the course of a person 's life and future, and it is not something to take lightly. I am saying this as a person with relative financial means ; I can not IMAGINE what it would have been like to experience this sort of emotional let down if I were unemployed or struggling to make ends meet. This sort of casual approach to communication is upsetting and unprofessional. 

It is also unacceptable how difficult it is to reach MOHELA. Even the hold experience was misleading -- I was told that the wait time would be 46 minutes at the start of the call, but the hold was actually 56 minutes. It's a small difference, but it adds up to make MOHELA seem unreliable and untrustworthy. It's also just an unacceptable amount of time to wait on hold. Experiencing long hold times and receiving inaccurate information from a federal student loan servicer is confusing and it makes it challenging for student borrowers to manage our finances and debt. MOHELA needs to hire more staff or take other action to be more responsive to borrowers. This is particularly important at a time when the company is likely receiving calls from countless borrowers who are confused and fearful that they will miss out on a life-changing rebate if they are unable to remain on hold.
Company public response: Company believes it acted appropriately as authorized by contract or law

Consumer complaint narrative:
I recently had {$3500.00} stolen from my account fraudulently and to put it lightly, the worst part about my situation was my experience with how TD Bank handled the incident rather than the fraud itself. 

On XX/XX/2021 at XXXX, I received a call from the real TD bank customer service number ( XXXX is what showed on my caller ID ). The Agent claimed to be XXXX with the TD Bank fraud department. XXXX with TD Bank advised that I had a few suspicious charges on my account from XXXX, New York. He asked for my permission to send these charges over to their fraud department and I said sure. All he needed me to do was reply Y to a series of normal cell phone text messages. 

At no point during this call did I visit TD Banks website, use their banking app, provide my private information to the caller, or request that funds be transferred out of my account in any way. The call ended with no other actions taking place. 

Unfortunately, what I did not know is that TD Bank had already granted this person access to my account without my involvement. 

I have now been told that the criminal used TD Banks XXXX service, a service that I have never used during my time with TD and did not use on that day either. The individual initiated twenty-three charges in total using TDs XXXX service. 

After the call, I went to check my account and realized something was fishy when I couldnt log into my mobile checking account. So I called the bank using the same number I always use. As instructed by TD, I notified the police of the event and have been working with a detective here in XXXX, MA. I have now submitted three fraudulent claims through TD customer service representatives and sent a record of the police report as well. The representative that I have been in touch with at my local TD branch has even submitted a letter of acknowledgment that my situation is legitimate. 

Furthermore, I have been calling customer service consistently to check up on the status of my claims and to ensure they have received all pieces of documentation to validate my case. Each time asking for my story to be repeated. I found out by way of calling customer service my claims of fraud have been denied. I have come to conclude that my story had been skewed and altered to fit TD Banks definition of a scam, even though the criminal clearly gained access to my account before ever calling me. 

TD Banks unwillingness to listen and the skewing of facts has made this a profoundly frustrating and long process.

Product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
On XX/XX/2023, I received a letter indicating I had opened an account with Robinhood. I had not opened an account with the company, and since they require XXXX to verify, it was clear that someone got my identity information and opened a fraudulent account with the company. Robinhood is the subject of this complaint. I began notifying Robinhood immediately on XX/XX/2023 that someone had opened an account in my name. I received no response to that email for several days, and the responses were not substantive. Following their requests, I filed an identity theft report with the FTC and I filed a local police report. I sent both documents to them immediately after filing. They appear to have done nothing with this information after requesting it, as I got no substantive response to this information. Getting more concerned, I tried to find a way to talk to someone on the phone. However, in order to talk to someone on the phone, you have to have an account with Robinhood. So I downloaded the app so I could speak to someone over the phone. But after multiple repeated email and phone contacts, including several times verifying my identity with all requested forms of doing so, I have been unable to get Robinhood to understand that whoeever is using my identity is doing so with criminal intent, and close down the fraudulent account. I have not been able to get substantive responses over email despite repeated attempts. Over the phone representatives have consistently directed me to take the call offline to resolve it over email, but then do not respond to my emails. Since they are not responding to my emails, and seem to be unwilling to discuss the matter with me over the phone, I appear to be stuck.

Meanwhile, more than a week later, I am still waiting for a resolution to this, while someone who has stolen my identity is using my information to run an account in my identity on the Robinhood app. Robinhood has all the necessary information to confirm that this is indeed criminal activity on their app and I have repeatedly told them this. Nonetheless, Robinhood has refused to hear me out or help me close down this account even though I have complied with all the various requests that they have made about filing reports and verifying my identity. I would like the company to assist a law-abiding person in protecting their identity from criminal misuse, close down the fraudulent account, and provide confirmation that this has been done.

Subject : Urgent Request for Assistance in Recovering Funds from Fraudulent Job Scam and Denied Claim by Wells Fargo Dear Consumer Financial Protection Bureau Team, I am writing to urgently seek your assistance regarding a fraudulent job scam that has caused me significant financial distress. I am a student, and the amount involved is a substantial portion of my finances during this critical period. I have exhausted efforts with my bank, Wells Fargo, to recover the funds, but unfortunately, my claim was denied after 60 days, leaving me with no resolution. 

Incident Details : On XX/XX/XXXX, I identified that a check I had deposited as part of a supposed job offer for a Salesforce Administrator role was counterfeit. The scam was well-orchestrated, beginning with an email from what appeared to be a legitimate company offering a job opportunity. I received a detailed job briefing and was instructed to install the Teams app for an interview. The interactions, including the email addresses and the individuals involved, appeared genuine and professional. 

After a successful virtual interview, I was informed that I had secured the position and was provided with a check for {$4500.00}, supposedly to purchase office supplies and a XXXX. I was instructed to deposit the check and wire a significant portion of the funds to a specified vendor for these purchases. This process repeated with additional requests for wire transfers for software and other expenses after I was provided with the checks for {$2600.00} and {$1800.00}. 

Checks were deposited to my account and below are the details : Between ( XX/XX/XXXX - XX/XX/XXXX ) Transaction Type : Check Deposit Amount of Transaction : {$4500.00} ; {$2600.00} ; {$1800.00} Recipients Bank : XXXX XXXX XXXX ( mobile check deposit ). 

I was asked to wire transfer the funds to the below account after the deposited check 's funds got available in my Bank. Below are the details : Between ( XX/XX/XXXX - XX/XX/XXXX ) Transaction Type : WIRE Amount of Transaction : {$1800.00} ; {$2600.00} ; {$2900.00} Recipients Bank : WELLS FARGO BANK , N.A . 

Each time, the funds appeared briefly available in my account before the checks were ultimately identified as counterfeit, resulting in a total loss of {$7400.00}. I immediately contacted my bank 's XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX ) check fraud division on XX/XX/XXXX, on the same day of identifying the check as counterfeit, and initiated a claim to recall the wire transfers. 

However, after a long wait of over 60 days, I received a notice from Wells Fargo ( recipient account where the funds were wire transferred to ) stating that my claim had been denied. This outcome is devastating, given the significant financial impact this has had on me. Despite acting quickly and reporting the fraudulent activity promptly, I feel I am not receiving the justice or support necessary from the bank in recovering my funds. 

Request for Assistance : I am respectfully requesting the CFPB 's intervention in this matter. I have all the documentation related to the scam, including the job offer emails, the counterfeit checks, wire transfer receipts, Consumer Report To The Federal Trade Commission and the claim denial notice from Wells Fargo. I will submit all these attachments for your review. 

I kindly urge you to review my case and take appropriate action to assist me in recovering my funds from Wells Fargo. The amount lost is a huge sum for me, and I am seeking justice and financial relief in this matter. 

Thank you for your time and assistance. I look forward to your response and any steps you can take to resolve this issue. 

Sincerely, XXXX Date : XX/XX/XXXX

This puts account holders at significant risk, even when they are vigilant. 
I respectfully request that the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau investigate this matter, including : How scammers are able to use bank numbers and impersonate representatives so effectively. 
What safeguards banks and financial institutions are required to implement to prevent consumers from being deceived in this manner. 
Whether my bank acted appropriately in handling my report of fraud and whether further protections, reimbursement, or regulatory action are warranted.

Transaction details of the fraudulent transfers. 
My communication with the bank after the incident. 
I appreciate your attention to this matter. This experience has caused me significant financial and emotional distress, and I urge the Bureau to hold institutions accountable for ensuring consumers are protected against these increasingly sophisticated fraud tactics.

Product: Credit card or prepaid card
Consumer complaint narrative:
My XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX cards were switched to Capital One last year ( XXXX ) I also had a Saks card and Capital One XXXX. 

Since XXXX I've received letters from Capital One re : the Saks and MC asking for " information '' they needed to update. So I called the number on the MC. They said the letter wasn't from Capital One and it was probably a scam. They said my accounts were fine.

A few months ago I logged onto the website and saw that the Saks card and MC were " currently restricted. '' Called Capital One again. They said they needed to " collect information '' for the account per federal law. Told them about the previous call when CSR told me the letter was a scam, They said she was wrong.

I updated the information and they removed the hold on the accounts. I thought my updated info would apply to all Capital One accounts since now it was in their system. 
Mais non. 

More letters! I thought I had already resolved the issue so I disregarded them. 
Forward to this morning : I logged onto the website and saw that the XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX cards are " currently restricted. '' Called Capital One ( again ) to ask about the restriction. Spoke with a XXXX I didn't understand. Transferred to another person I didn't understand. " Ma'am we are looking into it. '' Transferred 2 more times. Call time : 30 minutes and counting.

CSR told me I didn't respond to the letters. " We need to collect information about your account. '' Told her I called before and gave the information. " I can give you the information right now. Will that lift the restriction? '' Short answer : No.

They closed these 2 cards because I didn't answer the letter. 
" We also called you several times. '' Maybe so, but they never left a message. I get random junk calls all day long. How am I supposed to know Capital One is calling me if they don't leave a message? 

Now my accounts are closed. And they can't be reopened, because I'm past the 30-day response period. " Can I reapply? '' Chilly tone from CSR. " Well, you're welcome to do that if you want... I certainly can't stop you. '' That's customer retention right there. This wouldn't fly at my job.

Me to customer : I guess you can come back to our store if you really want to... I certainly can't stop you.

Manager to me : Sweetie, we've had some calls from corporate about you...

Also closing these cards has tanked my credit score. It's down by almost XXXX points. 
Thanks, Capital One!

Product: Credit card
Sub-product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
Issue: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
Sub-issue: Credit card company isn't resolving a dispute about a purchase on your statement
Consumer complaint narrative:
In response to my original complaint ( # XXXX ), the bank submitted a reply ( Attachment XXXX ) that fails to address any of the core allegations regarding its mishandling of my chargeback request and related misconduct. The banks response appears to exploit procedural gaps : the complaint was transferred from the FDIC before I could upload supporting documents, and several response boxes were unintentionally left unchecked during submission. 

Specifically, the bank avoided any rebuttal of my evidence that the merchant forged my signature and submitted fabricated documents in support of their case. Rather than confronting the facts, the bank repeats discredited claims including that my documentation did not allow them to pursue credit from the merchant while ignoring the substantive issues I raised in my original chargeback request ( Attachment XXXX ), my objections to the merchants false response ( Attachment XXXX ), and multiple follow-up complaints. 

The response once again refers to the forged contract and an alleged absence of a merchant refund promise as the basis for denial despite overwhelming evidence showing that these claims are false. My analysis, timely submitted to the bank ( Attachment XXXX ), shows clear contradictions between the emails I actually received and submitted with my original chargeback request ( Attachment XXXX ), and the email allegedly sent to me by the same merchant representative on XX/XX/year>, which was submitted only in the merchants response ( Attachment XXXX ). These inconsistencies strongly suggest that the latter was fabricated, thereby discrediting the entire set of merchant-provided documents. 

The consideration of my authorization for the payment as a verbal agreement to the terms of the later written contract is without legal basis, as it is not supported by any audio recording or transcript confirming the terms discussed during the telephone conversation. Citing it as a factor in denying my claim based solely on a statement from the merchant, who had already been shown to have submitted forged and fabricated documents is wholly inappropriate and undermines any claim of impartiality in the banks review process. 

The banks assertion that my questions to the Dispute Department ( Attachment XXXX ) were " unsubstantiated '' is demonstrably false. The faxed questions were successfully transmitted, as shown in the fax confirmation report ( Attachment XXXX ). While the fax number referenced in the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX response ( Attachment XXXX ) failed to accept transmissions, the number used in the Dispute Departments denial letter ( Attachments XXXX ) did accept them. 

The questions I asked were simple and direct, and essentially required the bank to answer : - Do the contradictions in the merchants emails constitute proof of fabrication? 
- If so, does this render the merchants contract and supporting documents invalid? 

These questions go to the XXXX of whether the bank knowingly sided with a fraudulent party. The bank avoided answering them despite receiving them twice : first via fax and then through a direct email reply ( Attachment XXXX ) likely because the only honest answers would confirm that the bank turned a XXXX eye to the fraud. 

Moreover, the banks assertion that it does not compensate for scams misrepresents the core issue. I am not seeking compensation for a scam. I am seeking accountability for the banks own role in mishandling my dispute including its failure to investigate the merchants fraud, its disregard of clear evidence, and its evasive conduct throughout the entire process. 

The banks actions include : - Delaying a formal dispute : On XX/XX/year>, a representative promised a reversal that never occurred. On XX/XX/year>, another rep refused to open a dispute unless I first contacted the merchant. On XX/XX/year>, a dispute was finally opened but without requesting my documents only to be closed on XX/XX/year> due to lack of documentation ( Attachment XXXX ). 
- Enabling scammers : On XX/XX/year>, a bank representative facilitated the scammers request for my signature and ID. 
- Refusing to investigate fraud : The bank accepted forged and fabricated documents without scrutiny and concealed the merchants fraud behind generic language ( Attachment XXXX ). 

These actions demonstrate that the bank treats scams not as fraud but as a legitimate business opportunity taking all necessary measures to secure its share of the scammers profits. 

I believe these actions constitute violations of Regulation Z ( 12 CFR 1026.13 ) regarding fair resolution of billing disputes, as well as unfair, deceptive, and abusive practices under the Dodd-Frank Act ( 12 U.S.C. 5531 ). The banks failure to evaluate clear evidence of fraud, reliance on fabricated documents, and refusal to answer critical questions reflect systemic abuse of its position in the dispute process. 

However, due to the forced arbitration clause in the cardholder agreement, and without intervention from the Bureau, the banks abusive and deceptive practices along with its blatant violations of applicable rules and regulations will go unpunished and are likely to continue.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.
